description:
**[CRITICAL CLEANUP]**

Per FRAC-21 §H2: the shadcn `Button` is unused by every product page AND its dependencies are broken. Real CTAs are raw `<a>` with no `focus-visible`.

## What's broken
- `src/components/ui/button.tsx` references undefined Replit-injected utilities/tokens: `hover-elevate`, `active-elevate-2`, `[border-color:var(--button-outline)]`, `border-primary-border`, `border-destructive-border`, `border-secondary-border`. None declared in `src/index.css`. **Hover/active elevation is a silent no-op; outline border is invisible.**
- Real CTAs are raw `<a className="hover:underline">` (e.g. `EventsPage.tsx:55,70`, `NeighborhoodPage.tsx:59`, `PoliticalClubPage.tsx:26`, `PeoplePage.tsx:29`, `Campus.tsx:130` defines its own `PrimaryButton`). Across all of pages/, sections/, layout/, house/, `focus-visible:` appears exactly TWICE.
- Navbar menu buttons (`Navbar.tsx:282-339`) have no `aria-label` and no focus-visible style.

## Decision required (open to user judgment)
1. **Fix Button** — define the missing tokens in `src/index.css`, then migrate all raw `<a>` CTAs to use Button or its `link`/`outline` variant.
2. **Retire Button** — delete `button.tsx` and the unused shadcn primitives it pulls in. Codify the raw-anchor CTA pattern as the system; add a `.cta` utility with proper focus state.
3. **New minimal Button** — write a small new Button matching current product reality (uppercase, mono, underline-on-hover), with focus-visible baked in. Migrate CTAs.

Recommend option 3 — closest to shipped reality, smallest surface area, gives FRAC-19 a real component to document.

## Acceptance criteria

- All product CTAs (raw `<a>` and Button-like) have visible focus-visible state
- Navbar menu icon buttons have `aria-label` and focus state
- Whichever option chosen: the broken Replit-token references in `button.tsx` are resolved (defined OR removed)
- No silent no-op hover/active classes remain

## Cites
FRAC-21 synthesis §H2
title: [Cleanup] Button strategy: pick one (fix Replit-broken shadcn Button, OR retire it, OR new minimal Button); ensure focus-visible on real CTAs
